message:
chore(deps): migrate asupersync + asupersync-tokio-compat to crates.io v0.3.0

Drops the sibling-path deps on `../asupersync` and
`../asupersync/asupersync-tokio-compat` in favor of the v0.3.0
published crates.io release. Matches the rationale in the old
comment: "remote RCH workers resolve the same synced path" — now
they resolve it from crates.io without needing the /dp symlink at
all.

- Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]:
  * `asupersync = { path = "../asupersync" }` →
    `asupersync = { version = "0.3.0" }`.
  * `asupersync-tokio-compat = { path =
    "../asupersync/asupersync-tokio-compat" }` →
    `asupersync-tokio-compat = { version = "0.3.0" }`.
  Refresh the inline comment to reflect the new crates.io-based
  resolution path.
- All vc_* workspace members consuming these via
  `asupersync = { workspace = true }` /
  `asupersync-tokio-compat = { workspace = true }` pick them up
  automatically.
- Cargo.lock: refreshed via `cargo update`.

files.patch:
@@ -23,11 +23,10 @@ license = "MIT"
 repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/vibe_cockpit"
 
 [workspace.dependencies]
-# Async runtime (primary)
-# Use the canonical sibling checkout so remote RCH workers resolve the same
-# synced path instead of relying on a host-local /dp symlink.
-asupersync = { path = "../asupersync" }
-asupersync-tokio-compat = { path = "../asupersync/asupersync-tokio-compat" }
+# Async runtime (primary) — from crates.io since the v0.3.0 release so
+# remote RCH workers and fresh clones resolve without a sibling checkout.
+asupersync = { version = "0.3.0" }
+asupersync-tokio-compat = { version = "0.3.0" }
 # Tokio kept as an explicit compat bridge while crate-local migrations land in
 # Phases 2a–2e. This is intentionally broad so each crate can compile until its
 # direct Tokio usage is removed.
